etymology.description: semantic 壺(壺) (hú, "jar") + phonetic 吉(吉) (jí, "lucky; auspicious") -> a complete or whole item -> reinforced meaning of "one"
etymology.interpretation: 壹(壹) (yī, "one") uses 壺(壺) (hú, "jar") as the semantic component suggesting entirety or completeness, while 吉(吉) (jí, "lucky; auspicious") provides the phonetic element. This character emphasizes a complete or singular unit, unlike 一(一) (yī, "one"). 壹 often appears in formal contexts to prevent alterations, such as in checks and legal documents.

examples.chinese: 他每天只喝壹杯茶(他每天只喝壹杯茶)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān zhǐ hē yī bēi chá.
examples.english: He only drinks one cup of tea every day.

examples.chinese: 他用壹只手就能提起那包东西(他用壹隻手就能提起那包東西)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng yī zhī shǒu jiù néng tí qǐ nà bāo dōngxī.
examples.english: He can lift that bag with one hand.

examples.chinese: 壹个人守着壹座空房子(壹個人守著壹座空房子)，感觉十分孤独(感覺十分孤獨)。
examples.pinyin: Yī gè rén shǒuzhe yī zuò kōng fángzi, gǎnjué shífēn gūdú.
examples.english: Guarding an empty house alone feels very lonely.
